clusters.title: Sherpa found alive on Mount Everest after six days missing
clusters.neutral_headline: Sherpa found alive on Mount Everest after six days missing
bullet_summary: A Nepali Sherpa has been found alive on the slopes of Mount Everest after missing for six days without food or oxygen
A Sherpa guide was found crawling to base camp on Mount Everest a week after he went missing
Cleaners found Dawa Sherpa crawling towards Base Camp six days after he went missing at a higher altitude

items.title: Sherpa found alive on Mount Everest after six days missing
items.standfirst: A Nepali Sherpa has been found alive on the slopes of Mount Everest after missing for six days without food or oxygen.
items.content: A Nepali Sherpa has been found alive on the slopes of Mount Everest after missing for six days without food or oxygen.

items.title: Missing Sherpa guide found alive on Everest after funeral rites had begun
items.standfirst: Climbing support team rescue Hillary Dawa Sherpa almost a week on from when he was last seenA Nepali guide who was believed to have died on Mount Everest has been found crawling to base camp a week after going missing – and after his funeral rites had begun.Dawa Sherpa, also known as Hillary Dawa Sherpa after the famous climber Edmund Hillary, was last seen on 29 May but did not reach base camp with other climbing groups. Continue reading...

Continue reading...

clusters.title: Senate Republicans vote to block motion to ban Trump’s ‘anti-weaponization’ fund – live
clusters.neutral_headline: Trump official-turned-foe John Bolton accepts plea deal in documents case
bullet_summary: Trump's former advisor turned critic had reached a deal with prosecutors, two people said
Reported by Guardian World, BBC World, CBS News and others

items.title: Senate Republicans vote to block motion to ban Trump’s ‘anti-weaponization’ fund – live
items.standfirst: Republicans vote against amendment to kill fund after acting attorney general Todd Blanche told lawmakers earlier this week that DOJ was ‘not moving forward with the fund’Sign up for the Breaking News US emailDonald Trump’s former national security adviser-turned-foe John Bolton is expected to plead guilty over mishandling classified documents, multiple outlets are reporting.According to CNN, which first reported the news citing three sources, Bolton intends to plead guilty to one count of illegal retention of sensitive national security documents, and has also agreed to pay a more than $2m fine. The New York Times hears the same, adding that he could face anywhere from no prison time to up to five years behind bars when he is sentenced. Continue reading...
items.content: Republicans vote against amendment to kill fund after acting attorney general Todd Blanche told lawmakers earlier this week that DOJ was ‘not moving forward with the fund’Sign up for the Breaking News US emailDonald Trump’s former national security adviser-turned-foe John Bolton is expected to plead guilty over mishandling classified documents, multiple outlets are reporting.According to CNN, which first reported the news citing three sources, Bolton intends to plead guilty to one count of illegal retention of sensitive national security documents, and has also agreed to pay a more than $2m fine. The New York Times hears the same, adding that he could face anywhere from no prison time to up to five years behind bars when he is sentenced. Continue reading...

items.content: Trump's former advisor turned critic had reached a deal with prosecutors, two people said.

items.title: Early details on John Bolton plea deal over mishandled classified documents, according to sources
items.standfirst: President Trump's former national security adviser, John Bolton, will plead guilty to one count of retaining classified national security information, two sources say. CBS News legal reporter Katrina Kaufman has more.
items.content: President Trump's former national security adviser, John Bolton, will plead guilty to one count of retaining classified national security information, two sources say. CBS News legal reporter Katrina Kaufman has more.

items.content: Despite serving as Trump's national security adviser, Bolton has emerged as a top critic of the president.

clusters.title: Trump family’s planned luxury resort sparks protests in Albania
clusters.neutral_headline: Kushner-backed luxury resort plan sparks protests in Albania
bullet_summary: Demonstrators oppose $4bn resort backed by investment firm founded by US president’s son-in-law Jared Kushner
Protests started end of May after videos showed bulldozers on the #beach

items.title: Trump family’s planned luxury resort sparks protests in Albania
items.standfirst: Demonstrators oppose $4bn resort backed by investment firm founded by US president’s son-in-law Jared Kushner
items.content: Demonstrators oppose $4bn resort backed by investment firm founded by US president’s son-in-law Jared Kushner

items.title: Albania: luxury complex project linked to Trump's family sparks outrage
items.standfirst: Anger is growing in #Albania over a plan to build luxury hotels and villas in a protected area on the #Adriatic coast. The project is being led by an investment #fund linked to #Trump's son-in-law Jared #Kushner. Protests started end of May after videos showed bulldozers on the #beach.
items.content: Anger is growing in #Albania over a plan to build luxury hotels and villas in a protected area on the #Adriatic coast. The project is being led by an investment #fund linked to #Trump's son-in-law Jared #Kushner. Protests started end of May after videos showed bulldozers on the #beach.

items.title: Kushner-backed luxury resort plan sparks protests in Albania
items.standfirst: Demonstrators say the project backed by Donald Trump's son-in-law would harm a protected environment.
items.content: Demonstrators say the project backed by Donald Trump's son-in-law would harm a protected environment.

items.title: Protests in Albania grow over Jared Kushner-backed luxury resort
items.standfirst: Conservation groups say work has begun in protected coastal area, while prime minister insists project will bring jobs and investment Protests in Albania over a proposed luxury resort backed by Donald Trump’s son-in-law, Jared Kushner, are set to intensify after opponents rejected an offer from the country’s prime minister “to discuss solutions”.Thousands took to the streets of Tirana for a third straight day on Wednesday, some of them brandishing inflatable flamingos in a nod to feared environmental damage, amid mounting calls for the project to be blocked. Continue reading...
items.content: Conservation groups say work has begun in protected coastal area, while prime minister insists project will bring jobs and investment Protests in Albania over a proposed luxury resort backed by Donald Trump’s son-in-law, Jared Kushner, are set to intensify after opponents rejected an offer from the country’s prime minister “to discuss solutions”.Thousands took to the streets of Tirana for a third straight day on Wednesday, some of them brandishing inflatable flamingos in a nod to feared environmental damage, amid mounting calls for the project to be blocked. Continue reading...

items.title: Marjane Satrapi, French-Iranian author of Persepolis, dies at 56
items.standfirst: French-Iranian author and illustrator Marjane Satrapi, best known for Persepolis, has died at 56, according to members of her entourage. The graphic memoir became an acclaimed book and film. [france24.com]

bullet_summary: Reported by BBC Top Stories, Al Jazeera, France24

items.title: How shock French Open finalist's tiny tattoo gives meaning to big run
items.standfirst: Qualifier Maja Chwalinska is one win away from an unthinkable French Open title after coming through the darkest period of her life.
items.content: Qualifier Maja Chwalinska is one win away from an unthinkable French Open title after coming through the darkest period of her life.

items.title: History-making qualifier Chwalinska sets up Andreeva French Open final
items.standfirst: Poland's Maja Chwalinska beats Diana Shnaider to reach French Open final and makes history after qualifying run.
items.content: Poland's Maja Chwalinska beats Diana Shnaider to reach French Open final and makes history after qualifying run.

items.title: Polish qualifier Maja Chwalinska carries fairytale run into French Open final
items.standfirst: Polish world number 114 Maja Chwalinska made French Open history on Thursday as she beat Russian Diana Shnaider in straight sets to become the first qualifier to go all the way to the final at Roland Garros – and only the second in Grand Slam history after Emma Raducanu's title run at the US Open in 2021.
items.content: Polish world number 114 Maja Chwalinska made French Open history on Thursday as she beat Russian Diana Shnaider in straight sets to become the first qualifier to go all the way to the final at Roland Garros – and only the second in Grand Slam history after Emma Raducanu's title run at the US Open in 2021.

clusters.title: Lebanon health ministry says Israeli strikes in south and east kill eight
clusters.neutral_headline: Hezbollah rejects Israel-Lebanon truce as Trump scrambles to end Iran war
bullet_summary: An Israeli strike in eastern Lebanon killed five people on Thursday, while another attack near the southern city of Tyre killed three more, the Lebanese health ministry said

items.title: Lebanon health ministry says Israeli strikes in south and east kill eight
items.standfirst: An Israeli strike in eastern Lebanon killed five people on Thursday, while another attack near the southern city of Tyre killed three more, the Lebanese health ministry said. The strikes also left eight wounded, including three children and two women. They came a day after the Lebanese and Israeli governments agreed a ceasefire plan for Israel's war with Hezbollah, which the group has rejected. FRANCE 24's Antonia Kerrigan reports from Beirut.
items.content: An Israeli strike in eastern Lebanon killed five people on Thursday, while another attack near the southern city of Tyre killed three more, the Lebanese health ministry said. The strikes also left eight wounded, including three children and two women. They came a day after the Lebanese and Israeli governments agreed a ceasefire plan for Israel's war with Hezbollah, which the group has rejected. FRANCE 24's Antonia Kerrigan reports from Beirut.

items.title: A 'ceasefire war' in Lebanon continues as 'chaotic global leadership' takes its toll
items.standfirst: Hezbollah's chief on Thursday rejected a conditional truce announced by Lebanese and Israeli envoys, demanding instead a comprehensive ceasefire and full Israeli withdrawal as he threatened northern Israel with new attacks. FRANCE 24's Noga Tarnopolsky reports from Jerusalem.
items.content: Hezbollah's chief on Thursday rejected a conditional truce announced by Lebanese and Israeli envoys, demanding instead a comprehensive ceasefire and full Israeli withdrawal as he threatened northern Israel with new attacks. FRANCE 24's Noga Tarnopolsky reports from Jerusalem.

items.title: Hezbollah rejects Israel-Lebanon truce as Trump scrambles to end Iran war
items.standfirst: Group calls ceasefire a ‘roadmap to annihilate part of the Lebanese people’, throwing regional peace talks into doubtHezbollah has rejected a US-brokered ceasefire plan agreed by the Lebanese and Israeli governments, throwing the future of a truce in Lebanon and regional peace negotiations into question.The group’s leader, Naim Qassem, called the plan a “roadmap to annihilate part of the Lebanese people” in a statement on Thursday. Continue reading...
items.content: Group calls ceasefire a ‘roadmap to annihilate part of the Lebanese people’, throwing regional peace talks into doubtHezbollah has rejected a US-brokered ceasefire plan agreed by the Lebanese and Israeli governments, throwing the future of a truce in Lebanon and regional peace negotiations into question.The group’s leader, Naim Qassem, called the plan a “roadmap to annihilate part of the Lebanese people” in a statement on Thursday. Continue reading...

items.title: Hezbollah rejects latest ceasefire agreement as Israeli strikes kill 4 in Lebanon
items.standfirst: Hezbollah on Thursday rejected the latest ceasefire agreement between Israel and the Lebanese government, and the militant group demanded a complete Israeli withdrawal from Lebanon as more fighting there hampered efforts to end the Iran war. The Hezbollah announcement came as Israeli strikes killed at least four people, according to local authorities, and a UN peacekeeper was killed in the crossfire. FRANCE 24's Renée Davis reports from Beirut, Lebanon.
items.content: Hezbollah on Thursday rejected the latest ceasefire agreement between Israel and the Lebanese government, and the militant group demanded a complete Israeli withdrawal from Lebanon as more fighting there hampered efforts to end the Iran war. The Hezbollah announcement came as Israeli strikes killed at least four people, according to local authorities, and a UN peacekeeper was killed in the crossfire. FRANCE 24's Renée Davis reports from Beirut, Lebanon.

clusters.title: Outrage in France as body discovered in case of missing 11‑year‑old Lyhanna
clusters.neutral_headline: Outrage in France as body discovered in case of missing 11‑year‑old Lyhanna
bullet_summary: A body was discovered on Thursday and formal identification was under way, an informed source said

items.title: Outrage in France as body discovered in case of missing 11‑year‑old Lyhanna
items.standfirst: Outrage erupted in France on Thursday after a body was discovered in the search for 11-year-old schoolgirl Lyhanna and it was revealed that the key suspect had been repeatedly accused of sexually abusing children but no action had been taken. The body has yet to be formally identified.
items.content: Outrage erupted in France on Thursday after a body was discovered in the search for 11-year-old schoolgirl Lyhanna and it was revealed that the key suspect had been repeatedly accused of sexually abusing children but no action had been taken. The body has yet to be formally identified.

items.standfirst: Interior minister announces review into handling of the cases after body reportedly found in search for 11-year-oldOutrage has erupted in France after it emerged the main suspect in the case of an 11-year-old girl missing since last week had been repeatedly accused of sexually abusing children with no action taken.A body was discovered on Thursday and formal identification was under way, an informed source said.
